methods.description: Process for synthesizing phosphors using pulverized coal furnace fly ash, diatomite, illite, carbon black, and Eu2O3 as starting materials, involving mixing and high-temperature treatment under nitrogen flow.

steps.description: Starting materials with molar ratio C:Si = 3.02 with Eu doping content 0.05 were weighed and mixed in a mortar [25].
steps.searchable_summary: weigh and mix starting materials (fly ash, diatomite, illite, carbon black, Eu2O3) at specified molar ratios

steps.description: Then, the starting powders were placed in alumina boats positioned in a box type furnace.
steps.searchable_summary: load mixed powder into alumina boats and place in furnace

steps.description: The reaction temperatures of different samples were 1350 • C, 1400 • C and 1450 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: heat furnace to specified reaction temperatures

steps.description: Then, the temperature was maintained for 3 h and 6 h under nitrogen flow (flow rate of 2 L/min), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain temperature for specified time under nitrogen flow

steps.description: The samples were cooled to room temperature in the furnace and characterized at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: cool samples to room temperature in the furnace

methods.description: Characterization of the phase composition and crystalline structure of samples using X-ray diffraction.

steps.description: The phase composition of the products was characterized by X-ray diffraction (XRD, DX-2700, Dandong, China). The XRD (Cu Kα1 radiation, 35 kV and 25 mA) patterns were obtained by scanning from 10 • to 80 • .
steps.searchable_summary: obtain XRD patterns using specified equipment and settings

steps.description: The step size was 0.1 • /s. The sampling time was 0.5 s and the data point interval (the step width of scanning) was 0.05 • .

methods.description: Determination of the Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR) of samples.

steps.description: The Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (FT-IR) of samples was determined by a Fourier-transform infrared spectrometer (NICOLET 380, Fitchburg, Wisconsin, USA) in the range of 400-1300 cm −1 with a spectral resolution of 2 cm −1 .
steps.searchable_summary: measure FT-IR spectra using specified equipment and settings

methods.description: Measurement of the photoluminescence properties of samples.

steps.description: The photoluminescence properties were measured by a fluorescence spectrophotometer (F-380, Tianjin, China).
steps.searchable_summary: measure photoluminescence properties using a fluorescence spectrophotometer

methods.description: Characterization of the morphology of the products by scanning electronic microscopy (SEM).

steps.description: The morphology of the products was characterized by scanning electronic microscopy (SEM, S-570 and JSM-IT300, Tokyo, Japan).

methods.description: Analysis of the chemical composition of the product by energy-dispersive spectroscopy (EDS).

steps.description: The chemical composition of the product was analyzed by energy-dispersive spectroscopy (EDS, JED-2300, Tokyo, Japan).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sample chemical composition using EDS

methods.description: Determination of the chemical compositions of starting materials using methods specified in the Chinese standard GB/T 14506−2010.

steps.description: The chemical compositions of these starting materials are listed in Table 1, and were detected by methods of chemical analysis for silicate rocks (Chinese GB/T 14506−2010).
steps.searchable_summary: determine chemical composition of starting materials using GB/T 14506−2010 standard
